title: calamagrostis purpurascens subsp. arctica
intro: Calamagrostis purpurascens subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic fescue, is a subspecies of perennial grass native to the Arctic and subarctic regions. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Calamagrostis purpurascens subsp. arctica include its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
